preamble.read_first: Read BUILD_LOG.md completely before touching a single file. The app is at Phase 7 — deploy ready — but has critical design, UX, and functional failures that must be resolved before this product is presentable to any client. This prompt is a COMPLETE OVERHAUL instruction. Treat every section as mandatory. Update BUILD_LOG.md after every single task. No exceptions.
preamble.context: The current build looks and feels like AI-generated slop. The design system was theoretically defined correctly but implemented poorly. The result is a flat, grey, lifeless interface that no agency principal in Qatar would pay QAR 2,500/month for. This prompt fixes that — permanently. The standard is: a 10,000 USD bespoke website. Every screen. No exceptions.

skills_activation.skill: gpt-taste — activate first. This is your inner critic. Every decision passes through it.
design-taste-frontend — translate taste into precise frontend code decisions.
high-end-visual-design — the visual quality floor everything must clear.
minimalist-ui — restraint filter. When in doubt, remove. Apply after building each screen.
stitch-design-taste — cross-component cohesion. Nothing feels disconnected.
image-to-code — close the gap between the mental image and the actual rendered output.
full-output-enforcement — every file ships complete. No truncation. No placeholders.
redesign-existing-projects — apply to every screen you revisit. Rebuild, never patch.

section.title: DESIGN FOUNDATION — JET BLACK AND GOLD
section.mandate: The current implementation is grey, warm-toned, and flat. It does not read as luxury. The new standard is: jet black with surgical gold lines. Sharp edges. Hard contrast. No warmth in the backgrounds. No softness. Think HSBC Private Bank meets a Formula 1 pit wall interface. Precision. Authority. Premium.

palette_overhaul.instruction: Replace globals.css entirely. The new palette below is absolute and final. Remove every trace of the warm washi palette, blossom tones, and soft greys.

typography.rule: Playfair Display for ALL headings and display text. Import weights 400 and 600.
DM Sans for ALL body, labels, buttons, and UI text. Import weights 400 and 500.
JetBrains Mono for ALL numbers, amounts, IDs, invoice figures. Weight 400 only.
Heading hierarchy: Display 56px/300lh/-0.02em tracking | H1 40px/1.12lh/-0.015em | H2 28px/1.22lh/-0.01em | H3 20px/1.3lh | Body 15px/1.7lh | Label 11px/500w/0.08em-tracking/UPPERCASE | Mono 14px
Key figures (totals, amounts, plan badges) = --gold-mid in JetBrains Mono.

section.title: LANDING PAGE TRANSFORMATION
section.mandate: The landing page must be the single most impressive thing a prospect sees. It is NOT a feature list. It is an experience. The product's professionalism lives inside the app — the landing page sells the feeling, the aspiration, and the demo. Every scroll should reveal something that makes the visitor lean forward.

dependencies.note: Use dynamic imports with SSR disabled for all Three.js components.

hero.description: Full viewport, absolute black (#000000), no gradients, no illustrations at start. Smooth scroll library (Lenis) initialized globally. Center-aligned layout.

elements.element: "Velocity AI — PortalOS" in 11px DM Sans, uppercase, 0.12em tracking, --gold-core color. A 32px 1px solid --gold-muted horizontal line on each side of the text. Fades in at 0ms delay.

Playfair Display, 72px desktop / 40px mobile, weight 400, --ink-primary. Two lines max: "The Agency Platform" / "Your Clients Will Brag About." Each word animates in with a staggered clip-path reveal: from `clip-path: inset(0 100% 0 0)` to `clip-path: inset(0 0% 0 0)`, 600ms, ease-out. Stagger: 40ms per word.
The 48px / 1px gold horizontal line. Animates width from 0 to 48px after headline. Appears BETWEEN headline and subheadline.
DM Sans, 18px, --ink-secondary, max 16 words. "Replace the email chains, Drive folders, and chasing clients — with one beautiful portal." Fade in 400ms after headline completes.

Two buttons side by side: Primary: "Try Agency View →" — gold button, dark text, --glow-gold-sm shadow. Secondary: "Try Client View →" — ghost button, white border. Below: "No credit card. No setup. Just click." in 11px --ink-tertiary. Animate up from y:16 after subheadline.

A Three.js scene rendered below the CTA group. Content: A slowly rotating 3D representation of the PortalOS interface — three floating glass-dark panels representing Agency View, Client Portal, and Invoice. Each panel is a flat box geometry with a dark material and gold wireframe edges. They orbit a central point slowly (0.003 rad/frame). Ambient light: dim. Point light: warm gold (#D4AF37) at position (2, 3, 2), intensity 1.2. On scroll: panels spread apart with GSAP ScrollTrigger (scrub: 1). Render with @react-three/fiber, dynamic import, SSR disabled. Canvas: width 100%, height 480px. Background: transparent (shows --bg-void behind).

Bottom of hero: "SCROLL" in 10px DM Sans uppercase, --ink-tertiary, letter-spacing 0.2em. A 40px animated line below it pulsing down: translateY(0→8px), 1.4s ease-in-out infinite. Gold color. Disappears after first scroll.

social_proof_bar.description: Full-width dark strip (--bg-surface) with 1px gold border top and bottom. Infinite horizontal marquee: fictional agency names as SVG wordmarks. Speed: 40px/s. Pause on hover. Clone the list for seamless loop. Label above: "Trusted by creative agencies across the Gulf."

problem_section.description: Section label: "──── THE PROBLEM" in gold. Headline: "Your clients deserve better than scattered Google Drive links." Three problem cards in a horizontal row. Each card: — --bg-surface background, 1px --border-hairline border, top-edge highlight. — A Lucide icon (24px, --gold-muted) top left. — Headline in Playfair Display H3. — Body in 15px DM Sans. — A thin 1px --gold-muted left border accent at the left edge of the card. Scroll reveal: cards stagger in (left card 0ms, center 100ms, right 200ms).

problems.p: Approvals over email. No record. No accountability.
Assets scattered across 5 tools. Nobody knows which is final.
Invoices sent via WhatsApp PDF. Chased manually. Paid late.

features_section.description: Alternating layout (image left / text right, then flip). Each feature: a dark browser mockup (built in HTML/CSS, not an image) on one side, headline + 3 bullet points + CTA link on the other. The mockup glows faintly gold on scroll entry (--glow-gold-xs added via IntersectionObserver class toggle). Features: Kanban Board, AI Brief Generator, Client Approval Portal, Invoicing. GSAP ScrollTrigger: on scroll, the mockup slides in from its direction (left or right) while the text fades up. Scrub: false. Trigger: 80% viewport.

demo_cta_section.description:
Full-width dark section, --bg-surface background. Centered layout. Above the headline: a 3D rotating gold cube (Three.js, 60px, slow rotation). Headline: "Experience it. Right now. No signup." Two large demo cards side by side:
Agency View card: — --bg-elevated, 1px --border-gold-dim, hover → --border-gold with --glow-gold-xs. — Icon: LayoutDashboard (Lucide, 32px, --gold-core). — Title: "Agency View" (Playfair H2). — Description: "Dashboard, Kanban, AI Briefs, Invoicing — everything the team touches." — Button: "Enter Agency View →" (gold primary). — Routes to /demo/agency.
Client View card: — Same card style. — Icon: Globe (32px, --gold-core). — Title: "Client Portal." — Description: "The branded experience your clients see. Approvals, files, invoices." — Button: "Enter Client Portal →" (ghost, gold border). — Routes to /demo/client.
Both cards float with a subtle CSS animation (keyframes: translateY 0→-6px→0, 4s ease-in-out infinite). Client card has a 600ms delay on the float, so they move independently.

testimonials.description: Dark section, 3 cards in a row. Each card: --bg-surface, 1px --border-hairline. Behind quote text: Playfair Display `"` at 96px, 6% opacity, --gold-core. Star rating: 5 SVG stars, --gold-core color, 14px. Quote text: 16px DM Sans italic --ink-secondary. Attribution: Avatar initials (--bg-elevated, --ink-primary) + Name (Playfair 500) + Agency + Role. Cards reveal with clip-path: inset(0 0 100% 0) → inset(0 0 0% 0) on scroll.

pricing.description: Three cards. Recommended (Pro) card: — 1px --border-gold, --inset-gold, --glow-gold-sm background glow. — "Most Popular" badge in --gold-core background with #000 text. — Price in JetBrains Mono, --gold-core, 40px. Other cards: flat --bg-surface. Monthly/Annual toggle: custom pill toggle. Annual shows "Save 20%" badge in --success-text.

faq.description: Radix UI Accordion. Each item: — 1px --border-hairline bottom border as divider. — Question: 16px DM Sans, --ink-primary, weight 500. — Answer: 15px DM Sans, --ink-secondary. — Icon: ChevronDown (Lucide), rotates 180° on open, --gold-core color. — Open state: question color becomes --gold-mid.

final_cta.description: --bg-void section. A single 1px --gold-muted horizontal line top and bottom. Centered: gold-rule, headline in Playfair 40px, subtext, two CTA buttons. No background imagery. No gradients. Just the black, the gold line, and the text. This restraint is intentional and powerful.

footer.description: --bg-void. Separated by 1px --border-hairline only. Logo left. Nav links center. Social icons right (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram). Social icons: Lucide icons, --ink-tertiary, hover → --gold-core, 200ms transition. Bottom strip: "© 2026 Velocity AI. All rights reserved." | "Privacy" | "Terms" All in 12px DM Sans, --ink-tertiary.

scroll_behavior.instruction: Install Lenis for smooth scroll. Initialize in a client component wrapper at root layout. Use GSAP ScrollTrigger for the 3D scene and feature mockups. Use IntersectionObserver for all .reveal elements (add .in-view class when 20% visible). The stagger script: after DOM ready, query all [data-reveal] elements, set their animation-delay based on data-delay attribute (in ms).

sidebar_overhaul.instruction:
The sidebar must feel architectural, not generic. Background: --bg-surface. Left edge: 1px solid --border-hairline. Right edge of sidebar: 1px solid rgba(212,175,55,0.08) — a barely-visible gold line separating sidebar from content. This is the first gold line the eye hits when looking at the app. It should feel intentional.
Logo area: PortalOS wordmark in Playfair Display, --ink-primary, 18px weight 600. A 1px --gold-muted horizontal line below the logo area.
Nav items: — Default: 13px DM Sans, --ink-tertiary, 40px height, 8px border-radius, 16px side padding. — Hover: background rgba(255,255,255,0.04), --ink-secondary, 200ms. — ACTIVE: background var(--gold-dim), --ink-primary, left border 2px solid --gold-core, text in --gold-mid, icon color --gold-core.
Icon set: REPLACE all Lucide icons in the sidebar navigation with Phosphor Icons (npm install @phosphor-icons/react). Use the Duotone weight for active items, Regular weight for inactive. Phosphor icons are significantly more distinctive than Lucide for navigation.
Notification bell: badge in --gold-core background, #000 text, count. Agency identity block at bottom: card with top-edge highlight, gold border on hover.

icons_overhaul.instruction:
The current Lucide-only icon set looks generic everywhere. Implement a two-library icon strategy:
— Navigation and structural UI: Phosphor Icons (Duotone weight, --gold-core color when active). npm install @phosphor-icons/react
— Status and semantic icons: Lucide React (kept for inline status badges and data).
— Feature icons (empty states, feature cards, onboarding): Custom SVG inline icons. Each empty state gets a bespoke single-path SVG illustration drawn in --gold-muted. These must look hand-crafted, not clip-art. Examples: Empty projects: a single geometric diamond outline, 48px. Empty comments: a minimal chat bubble with one dot, 48px. Empty deliverables: a minimalist file with one corner folded, 48px. Empty invoices: a minimal receipt with a gold checkmark, 48px.
Replace ALL navigation icons in agency-shell.tsx with Phosphor equivalents: Dashboard → HouseSimple (Phosphor) Clients → Buildings (Phosphor) Projects → SquaresFour (Phosphor) Invoices → Invoice (Phosphor) Settings → GearSix (Phosphor) Notifications → Bell (Phosphor) User → UserCircle (Phosphor)

dashboard_overhaul.instruction:
The dashboard must feel like a war room. Every metric has weight.
Stat cards: 4 cards top row. — --bg-surface background, 1px --border-hairline border, top-edge highlight. — Number: JetBrains Mono, 36px, --gold-mid color. — Label: 11px DM Sans uppercase, --ink-tertiary, 0.08em tracking. — Trend chip: tiny pill badge (+ or - percentage vs last 30 days). — Numbers count up from 0 on mount (framer-motion useMotionValue + useSpring, 800ms). — On hover: card lifts 3px, border becomes --border-gold-dim, --glow-gold-xs appears.
Activity feed: real-time, Ably powered. — Each entry: avatar initials (--bg-elevated, 32px, 6px radius) | action text | timestamp. — New entries slide in from top with opacity 0→1 + translateY(-8px→0), 300ms. — "Just now" timestamps pulse gold once on entry.
Pending approvals widget: — Title in Playfair H3. — Each row: client logo | deliverable name | "Waiting X days" in --danger-text if >3 days. — "Send Reminder" button: ghost, 32px height, on hover border becomes --border-gold-dim. — If 0 pending: empty state with the custom SVG, not a blank area.

project_workspace_overhaul.instruction:
The project workspace is the heart of the app. It must feel premium and purposeful.
PROJECT HEADER: — Full-width. --bg-surface. 1px --border-hairline bottom. — Project name: Playfair Display, 32px, --ink-primary. Inline editable on click. — Client badge: pill with client logo (16px) + name. --bg-elevated, 1px --border-subtle. — Status dropdown: custom, not native select. Options styled with semantic colors. — Due date: calendar icon (Phosphor) + date. Color changes: >7 days: --ink-tertiary | 3-7 days: --warning-text | <3 days: --danger-text. — Team avatars: stacked, 32px, 6px radius, --bg-elevated. "+N more" chip if >4. — Right side: "⋮" menu (Phosphor DotsThreeVertical) → Archive, Duplicate, Export.
TAB NAVIGATION: — 5 tabs: Board | Brief | Deliverables | Comments | Invoice. — Active tab: --ink-primary, 1px solid --gold-core bottom border (not a background fill). — Inactive: --ink-tertiary, hover → --ink-secondary. — Tab bar has 1px --border-hairline bottom border full width. — Tab transition: active border slides from previous to current tab (CSS transition on left/width).
KANBAN BOARD (Board tab): — Column headers: status name in 11px DM Sans uppercase, dot indicator (color-coded), task count chip. — Column background: --bg-sunken with 1px --border-hairline sides. Not a card — a trough. — Task cards: --bg-surface, 1px --border-hairline, 8px radius. On hover: gold border appears, lifts 2px. — While dragging: card gets --border-gold, --glow-gold-sm, scale(1.02), --shadow-xl. — Priority indicator: 3px left border (not a dot): URGENT: --danger-text | HIGH: --warning-text | MEDIUM: --gold-muted | LOW: --ink-ghost — Add task inline: bottom of each column, "+" button, 11px DM Sans uppercase, --ink-tertiary. Expands into an inline input on click (no modal for quick adds). — Filter bar: assignee avatars (click to filter), priority pills. Active filter gets gold border.
DELIVERABLES TAB — OVERHAUL: — Upload zone: dashed border (--border-gold-dim dashed), dark center, gold icon. On drag-over: border becomes solid --border-gold-hot, background becomes --gold-dim. — Each deliverable: full-width row, --bg-surface, 1px --border-hairline bottom. Left: file type icon (custom SVG per type, not generic Lucide File). Center: filename (--ink-primary), size + uploaded by + date (--ink-tertiary 12px). Right: status badge + "Request Approval" button + version chip + "⋮" menu. — Version history: clicking the version chip opens a popover showing all versions with timestamps and uploader. — Status badges follow the canonical badge system. — "Request Approval" button: gold primary, 32px height. Triggers action AND shows a success toast: "Approval request sent to [client name]." THIS BUTTON MUST WORK. Wire it to the deliverables server action. After clicking: button changes to "Approval Pending" (disabled, --warning-text color).
COMMENTS TAB: — Comment input pinned to bottom of tab panel. — "Internal only" toggle: a gold pill toggle (DM Sans 11px, uppercase). When ON: input background becomes rgba(212,175,55,0.04), left border 2px --gold-core. Internal comments have a gold lock icon (Phosphor Lock, 12px) before the avatar. Client users NEVER see internal comments. — Each comment: avatar | name + role + timestamp | body | reaction row. Reactions: a "+" button that opens a tiny emoji picker (6 options max). — New comment entry: fade in from opacity:0 + translateY(-4px), 200ms. Real-time via Ably. — Thread replies: indented 24px, with a 1px --border-hairline left thread line.
INVOICE TAB: — Invoice number: JetBrains Mono, --gold-mid, 13px. Auto-generated format: INV-2026-001. — Line items table: proper table element. Headers: 11px DM Sans uppercase, --ink-tertiary, 0.08em tracking. Cells: 15px DM Sans, --ink-secondary. Amounts: JetBrains Mono, --ink-primary, right-aligned. — Subtotal / Tax / TOTAL row: TOTAL: JetBrains Mono, 24px, --gold-mid, with a 1px --border-gold-dim top rule. — Action buttons row: "Save Draft" (ghost) | "Send to Client" (gold primary) | "Mark Paid" | "Void". — Status state machine: only show relevant buttons for current status. Paid status: show the amount in --success-text with a checkmark, no action buttons.

section.title: NOTIFICATION INBOX — AGENCY AND CLIENT
section.mandate: Both the agency side and client portal need a full notification inbox. This is not optional. It is a core UX feature that makes the product feel alive.

agency_notifications.bell_behavior: — Bell icon (Phosphor Bell) in the header. Shows a gold badge with unread count. — Clicking the bell opens a full-height slide-in panel from the RIGHT side of the screen. Width: 380px. Background: --bg-elevated. 1px --border-hairline left border. Animated: slides in from translateX(380px) to translateX(0), 250ms --ease-out. A backdrop overlay (rgba(0,0,0,0.40)) covers the rest of the app, click to close.

inbox_panel.header: "Notifications" in Playfair H2, --ink-primary. Unread count chip in --gold-core background. "Mark all read" button (ghost, right-aligned). 1px --border-hairline bottom divider. Close button (Phosphor X, 20px) top-right.
inbox_panel.filter_tabs: All | Unread | Approvals | Comments | Invoices Tab styles: same as main workspace tabs but smaller (12px).
inbox_panel.notification_items: Each notification item: — Full width, 72px min-height, --bg-surface on hover, 200ms. — Unread: 3px left border --gold-core + slightly lighter background. — Read: no border, no background change. — Left: type icon in a 36px circle (--bg-elevated). Icon color: --gold-mid. — Center: title (14px DM Sans weight 500, --ink-primary) + body preview (13px, --ink-tertiary, 1 line clamped). — Right: relative timestamp (12px, --ink-tertiary) + unread dot (6px, --gold-core). — Clicking: marks as read (dot disappears, left border fades out), navigates to the relevant resource. — 1px --border-hairline bottom divider between items.
inbox_panel.empty_state: Phosphor Bell icon (48px, --ink-ghost) + "You're all caught up." in Playfair H3 + "New activity will appear here in real time." in 14px DM Sans --ink-tertiary.
inbox_panel.real_time: Subscribe to Ably channel `notifications:agency:[agencyId]`. On new notification event: prepend to list with slide-down animation + increment badge count. The bell icon briefly pulses (scale 1→1.2→1, 300ms) when a new notification arrives.

client_notifications.instruction:
The client portal gets its own notification bell in the portal header. Same slide-in panel pattern, but scoped to client-relevant events only: — New deliverable uploaded for your review — Brief sent for your review — Invoice sent — payment due [date] — Agency left a comment on [Project] — Your approval was recorded ✓
Client notification bell badge: same gold style. Ably channel: `notifications:client:[clientId]`. All notifications deep-link to the relevant portal screen.

server_implementation.instruction:
Create actions/notifications.ts with: — createNotification(type, title, body, link, recipientId, recipientType) server action. — markAsRead(notificationId) server action. — markAllAsRead(agencyId | clientId) server action. — getNotifications(recipientId, recipientType, filter?) — returns typed notification list.
Wire createNotification into: — actions/comments.ts — on comment create → notify relevant parties. — actions/deliverables.ts — on requestApproval → notify client. — actions/deliverables.ts — on approve/reject → notify agency. — actions/invoices.ts — on send → notify client. On status change → notify agency. — actions/briefs.ts — on send-to-client → notify client.
Also publish an Ably event to the recipient's notification channel on every createNotification call.

section.title: CLIENT PORTAL REBUILD
section.mandate: The client portal is what clients experience. It must be the most polished screen in the product. The client has no interest in PM jargon. They want: clarity, beauty, and ease. Every button must be visible. Every action must be obvious. The approval button MUST work. This is a P0 bug.

portal_home.instruction:
Header: agency logo (from agency branding settings) + agency name in Playfair Display. 1px --border-gold-dim bottom border on header. The header brand color is pulled from agency.brand_color and applied as the gold accent override for this portal: :root { --gold-core: [agency.brand_color]; } — THIS FIXES THE BRAND COLORS BUG.
Hero greeting card: --bg-surface, --inset-gold, full width. "Welcome back, [Client Company Name]." in Playfair Display 36px. Subtext: "Here's where your projects stand today." in 16px DM Sans --ink-secondary.
Outstanding invoice banner: shown if any invoices are unpaid. Background: --warning-bg. Border: 1px solid --warning-border. Amount in JetBrains Mono --gold-bright. "Pay Now →" button gold primary. THIS BANNER MUST BE VISIBLE AND FUNCTIONAL.
Projects grid: 2-column grid on desktop. Each project card: — --bg-surface, 1px --border-hairline, top-edge highlight, 10px radius. — Hover: --border-gold-dim, lifts 2px, --glow-gold-xs. — Project name: Playfair H3. Client name: 12px DM Sans --ink-tertiary. — Progress bar: thin 4px bar. Gold fill for completion percentage. — Pending items count chip: "3 items need your attention" in --warning-text if any pending. — "View Project →" link at bottom right, gold colored, hover underline.
Nav: Projects | Invoices | [Notification Bell] | [Sign Out] Nav links: DM Sans 14px, --ink-tertiary, hover → --ink-primary. Active: --ink-primary, 1px --border-gold-dim bottom.

client_project_view.mandate: THIS IS THE MOST CRITICAL FIX. The current view is dull and non-functional. The client project view must have every action visible, every button working, and the layout must feel premium without agency-internal complexity.
client_project_view.tabs: Board is hidden from clients. They see: Brief | Deliverables | Comments | Invoice

brief_tab.instruction: Show the brief as a beautifully formatted read-only document. NOT a raw JSON dump. Parse the TipTap JSON and render proper HTML. H1-H3 in Playfair Display. Body in DM Sans. Status banner at top: "Draft" / "Awaiting Your Review" / "Approved ✓" "Approve Brief" button (gold primary, full width on mobile) if status = SENT. THIS BUTTON MUST WORK — wire to a client-side brief approval action.

deliverables_tab.instruction:
THIS IS WHERE THE APPROVAL BUTTON FIX IS REQUIRED.
Each deliverable gets a full card: — --bg-surface, 1px --border-hairline, 12px radius. — File preview: image → actual thumbnail. PDF → first-page render or PDF icon. Video → poster frame. Other → large file type icon. — File name (Playfair H3) + size + upload date. — Status badge (large, prominent, 14px DM Sans). — VERSION BADGE: "v2" chip if multiple versions exist.
ACTION BUTTONS — ALWAYS VISIBLE, NEVER HIDDEN: Two prominent buttons at the bottom of each deliverable card:
[✓ Approve] [Request Changes] Gold primary button. Ghost button, --danger-text border on hover.
"Approve" onClick: 1. Opens confirmation modal: "Are you sure you want to approve [filename]?" Modal has: Cancel (ghost) + "Yes, Approve" (gold primary). 2. On confirm: calls approveDeliverable server action. 3. Shows success toast: "Approved ✓ — [agency name] has been notified." 4. Status badge updates to "Approved" without page reload (optimistic update). 5. Triggers createNotification for agency users. 6. Button changes to "Approved ✓" (disabled, --success-text, checkmark icon).
"Request Changes" onClick: 1. Opens modal with textarea: "Describe the changes needed..." (required). 2. "Send Feedback" gold primary button. Disabled until textarea has content. 3. On submit: calls rejectDeliverable server action with feedback text. 4. Shows toast: "Feedback sent. The agency will be in touch." 5. Status updates to "Changes Requested". Button changes to "Changes Requested" (disabled).
FIX THE APPROVAL ACTION: In actions/deliverables.ts, ensure approveDeliverable: — Accepts deliverableId and clientUserId. — Updates deliverable status to APPROVED, sets approvedBy and approvedAt. — Creates a notification for all agency users. — Publishes Ably event to project channel. — Revalidates the portal project path.
FIX rejectDeliverable similarly.

comments_tab.instruction: Client sees only external comments (is_internal = false). Comment input at bottom: clean, simple. "Leave a comment..." placeholder. "Send" button: gold primary, 36px height. Comments display: avatar (initials in --bg-elevated) | name | timestamp | body. New comments appear in real time via Ably. Thread replies indented with 1px --border-hairline left line.

invoice_tab.instruction:
Show invoice as a beautifully formatted document. Agency logo top right. "INVOICE" in Playfair 40px, --gold-mid, top left. Invoice number in JetBrains Mono below the title. Agency details + client details in two columns. Line items table: proper HTML table, alternating row shading (--bg-surface / --bg-elevated). Amounts in JetBrains Mono. TOTAL line: Playfair Display 24px, --gold-mid, heavy gold line above it. Due date: large and prominent. Color: overdue → --danger-text.
"Pay Now" button: gold primary, FULL WIDTH on mobile, large (48px height). Links to Stripe Checkout. THIS BUTTON MUST ALWAYS BE VISIBLE if invoice is unpaid. "Download PDF" button: ghost, secondary. Generates PDF via @react-pdf/renderer.
If invoice is paid: show a large "PAID ✓" stamp in --success-text, Playfair 40px, rotated -15 degrees, with a 2px --success-text border around it. Paid date in JetBrains Mono below.

section.title: BRAND COLOR SYSTEM FIX
section.bug_description: When an agency sets their brand color in Settings > Branding, the color does not apply to the client portal. This is a P0 bug.

fix.instruction:
In the portal layout (app/(client)/portal/[clientSlug]/layout.tsx): 1. Fetch the agency record by clientSlug → client.agency_id → agency.brand_color. 2. Inject a style tag into the layout head: `style={{ '--gold-core': agency.brand_color } as React.CSSProperties}` applied to the root div of the portal layout. 3. This overrides --gold-core for the entire portal with the agency's brand color. 4. All gold-accented elements (active nav, buttons, borders, badges) automatically take the agency's brand color.
Also fix the branding settings page: — The color picker must update in real time. — The live preview card in the settings page must use the picked color as --gold-core. — On save: updateAgencyBranding server action updates agency.brand_color in DB. — After save: revalidate all portal paths for this agency. — Show a success toast: "Brand color updated. Client portals updated immediately."

portal_load_fix.instruction: The mock portal fails to load after setup. Debug and fix: 1. In middleware.ts: verify the subdomain rewrite handles localhost correctly in dev mode. In dev, [agency-slug].localhost:3000 should rewrite to /portal/[agency-slug]. 2. In the portal layout: add a notFound() call if the clientSlug resolves to no agency. 3. Ensure the dev bypass mock data includes a valid clientSlug that matches the demo route /portal/northstar-brand. 4. Add an explicit route for /portal as a redirect to /demo/client in dev mode. 5. Test: GET /portal/northstar-brand returns 200 with full portal HTML.

global_scroll.instruction: Create components/shared/smooth-scroll-provider.tsx — a client component that initializes Lenis smooth scroll on mount and exposes it via context. Wrap the root layout in this provider. All anchor links must work with Lenis scrollTo.

landing_3d_hero.instruction: Three.js scene in the hero (already specced in Section 2). Three floating panels representing the product. Panel material: MeshStandardMaterial, color: #0F0F0F, roughness: 0.9, metalness: 0.1. Panel wireframe: separate EdgesGeometry with LineBasicMaterial, color: #D4AF37, opacity: 0.4. Gold point light at (2, 3, 2), intensity 1.5. Ambient light intensity: 0.2. Panels auto-rotate on Y axis (0.002 rad/frame) and slightly on X (0.0005). On scroll: GSAP ScrollTrigger maps scroll progress to panel spread: start → panels close together (x: -1, 0, 1) middle → panels spread (x: -3, 0, 3) + slight Y rotation end → panels fade out (opacity → 0)

scroll_reveal_system.instruction:
Create hooks/use-reveal.ts — an IntersectionObserver hook that adds class 'in-view' to elements when they enter the viewport (threshold: 0.15).
Apply globally to all [data-reveal] elements via a useEffect in the root layout.
For staggered children, read data-delay attribute (ms): element.style.animationDelay = element.dataset.delay + 'ms';
CSS already defined in globals.css (Section 1).
Apply to: feature sections, testimonial cards, pricing cards, FAQ items, problem cards, stat cards on dashboard.

kanban_3d_drag.instruction: When a task card is being dragged: — Apply CSS transform: perspective(800px) rotateX(2deg) rotateY(-1deg) scale(1.04) — This gives the card a subtle 3D tilt as if it's been lifted off a table. — Add via DragOverlay in kanban-board.tsx: the overlay card gets a 'is-dragging' class. — Shadow: 0 20px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.6), 0 0 20px rgba(212,175,55,0.25). — This is a CSS-only 3D effect, no Three.js needed here.

modal_animations.instruction: All modals and dialogs: — Backdrop: fade in, opacity 0→0.85, 200ms. — Panel: scale 0.95→1.00 + opacity 0→1, 200ms, ease-out. — On close: reverse, 150ms. Use Framer Motion AnimatePresence in all Dialog components.

notification_panel_animation.instruction: The notification slide-in panel: — Slides from translateX(400px) to translateX(0), 280ms, ease-out. — Backdrop fades in simultaneously. — On close: slides back to translateX(400px), 200ms. Use Framer Motion AnimatePresence + motion.div.

micro_interactions.instruction:
Every interactive element must respond to user input immediately.
Buttons: scale(0.97) on mousedown, back to scale(1) on mouseup. 120ms ease.
Input fields: on focus, the border animates from --border-hairline to --border-gold-hot. The gold glow (--glow-gold-xs) appears simultaneously. 200ms ease.
Nav items: a 2px --gold-core left border slides in from height 0% to 100% on active.
Badge count on notification bell: when a new notification arrives, the badge does a quick scale(1→1.3→1) pop in 300ms.
Cards on hover: translateY(-2px) + border brightens. Both 200ms. GPU-composited only (transform and border-color, never width/height changes).
Toggle switches: smooth translate of the thumb, 200ms. Gold background when on.
Approval button after click: button content transitions from text to a checkmark icon with a 200ms crossfade. Background transitions from --gold-core to --success-bg.

toast_system.instruction:
Install: npm install sonner Replace any existing toast implementation with Sonner. Configure in root layout: Toaster with dark theme, position bottom-right. Custom styles: Background: --bg-elevated Border: 1px solid --border-hairline Border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10) Text: --ink-primary Success icon: --success-text Error icon: --danger-text Action button: --gold-core text, ghost style
Toast calls (ensure all server actions trigger these): — Approval success: toast.success("Approved ✓", { description: "Agency notified." }) — Rejection sent: toast.success("Feedback sent", { description: "Changes requested." }) — Save success: toast.success("Saved") — Error: toast.error("Something went wrong", { description: error.message }) — Reminder sent: toast.success("Reminder sent to [client name]")

command_palette.instruction: Install: npm install cmdk Create components/agency/command-palette.tsx. Triggered by Cmd+K (or Ctrl+K). Shows: recent projects, clients, quick actions (new project, new client, new invoice). Search filters results in real time. Style: --bg-elevated, 1px --border-gold-dim border, --shadow-xl. Search input: 18px DM Sans, --ink-primary, no border. Result items: Phosphor icon + label + type badge. Active result: --gold-dim background, --ink-primary text. Keyboard navigation: ArrowUp/Down to move, Enter to select, Escape to close.

select_component.instruction: Replace ALL native HTML select elements with a custom Radix UI Select component. Style to match the design system: — Trigger: same style as Input, with ChevronDown (Phosphor) icon. — Content: --bg-elevated, 1px --border-subtle, --shadow-lg, 8px radius. — Items: 14px DM Sans, --ink-secondary, 36px height, 8px side padding. Hover: --neutral-bg background, --ink-primary text. Selected: --gold-dim background, --gold-mid text, Check icon (Phosphor) right-aligned. This fixes the dull native select elements throughout the app.

avatar_component.instruction: Create components/ui/avatar.tsx. Props: name (string), size (sm/md/lg), src (optional image URL). If src: show image, object-cover, full border-radius. If no src: show initials (first letter of each word, max 2). Background: --bg-elevated. Text: --ink-primary. Border: 1px solid --border-subtle. Sizes: sm=24px/10px text, md=32px/13px text, lg=40px/16px text. Stacked avatars: margin-left -8px on each, hover spreads them apart.

section.title: WHAT NEVER TO DO

banned_colors.ban: rgba backgrounds with any blue, purple, or pink undertone
Warm beige/washi/paper tones — the old palette is gone
#FFD700 — cheap gold. Only --gold-core (#D4AF37) and its scale
Multi-color gradients as backgrounds or button fills
Pure #000000 as a card or surface color — use the void/base/surface scale

banned_patterns.ban: Colored icon backgrounds (red for dashboard, green for money) in sidebar
Floating 3D Spline embed — our own Three.js scenes only
Arrow-tip tooltip bubbles — flat tooltip, 6px radius, no arrow
Progress bars with gradient fills — solid gold only
Star emoji ratings — SVG stars only
White text on gold button — always dark (#000000) text on gold background
Any font other than Playfair Display, DM Sans, or JetBrains Mono
Placeholder comments in code: "// ... rest of implementation"
Truncated file output — every file ships complete (full-output-enforcement)

section.self_audit: Before shipping any screen, answer YES to all: [ ] Does this look like it cost $10,000 to design? [ ] Are the borders sharp and deliberate? [ ] Is gold used in 3 or fewer places on this screen? [ ] Are all action buttons fully functional? [ ] Do animations feel physical and earned, not decorative? [ ] Would a luxury brand art director approve this layout? [ ] Does every empty state have a custom illustration, never a blank area? [ ] Is the notification system wired and live? [ ] Does the approval button work end-to-end? If any answer is NO — rebuild that element before moving on.

section.title: OVERHAUL BUILD ORDER
section.instruction: Update BUILD_LOG.md at the START and END of every task below. Do not skip tasks. Do not reorder without documenting in BUILD_LOG.md.

phase.name: Foundation fixes — must be first
phase.task: A.1 Replace globals.css with new Obsidian v3 palette (Section 1)
A.2 Install all new packages: @phosphor-icons/react, @react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei, three, @types/three, gsap, @gsap/react, lenis, sonner, cmdk
A.3 Fix brand color injection in portal layout (Section 6)
A.4 Fix portal load bug — middleware subdomain rewrite (Section 6)
A.5 Fix approval server action end-to-end (Section 5)

phase.name: Agency app interior
phase.task: B.1 Sidebar overhaul — Phosphor icons, gold active state, gold separator line
B.2 Dashboard overhaul — stat cards, count-up, activity feed
B.3 Project workspace header and tab navigation overhaul
B.4 Kanban board overhaul — 3D drag, priority borders, inline add
B.5 Deliverables tab overhaul — working approval buttons, version history
B.6 Comments tab overhaul — internal toggle, threading, reactions
B.7 Invoice tab overhaul — formatted table, large total, Stripe pay
B.8 Replace all native select elements with Radix Select

phase.name: Notification system
phase.task: C.1 Build notification server actions and DB integration
C.2 Wire createNotification into all relevant actions
C.3 Build agency notification panel (slide-in drawer)
C.4 Build client notification panel
C.5 Wire Ably real-time to both notification panels

phase.name: Client portal rebuild
phase.task: D.1 Portal home — branded header, greeting card, projects grid
D.2 Client project view — all 4 tabs rebuilt per Section 5
D.3 Approval flow — both approve and request-changes fully working
D.4 Invoice view — formatted document, Pay Now button, paid stamp

phase.name: Landing page
phase.task: E.1 Lenis smooth scroll provider — root layout
E.2 Scroll reveal system — IntersectionObserver hook
E.3 Hero section — headline animation, gold rule, CTA buttons
E.4 Three.js floating panels scene — dynamic import, SSR disabled
E.5 Social proof marquee, problem section, features alternating
E.6 Demo CTA section — floating cards, agency/client view buttons
E.7 Testimonials, pricing, FAQ, final CTA, footer

phase.name: Polish and micro-interactions
phase.task: F.1 Sonner toast system — replace all existing toasts
F.2 Command palette (Cmd+K)
F.3 Page transitions — Framer Motion AnimatePresence
F.4 Modal animations — scale + fade
F.5 Kanban 3D drag tilt effect
F.6 Float animations on demo cards
F.7 All micro-interactions — button press, input focus, badge pop
F.8 Avatar component — replace all initials with the canonical component
F.9 Custom SVG empty state illustrations — one per empty context

phase.name: Verify and ship
phase.task: G.1 npx tsc --noEmit — zero errors required
G.2 npx next lint — zero warnings required
G.3 npx next build — clean build required
G.4 End-to-end test: demo/agency → create project → upload deliverable → demo/client → approve → invoice pay
G.5 Notification test: trigger a comment → verify bell badge updates in real time on both sides
G.6 UPDATE BUILD_LOG.md to OVERHAUL COMPLETE

section.title: HANDOFF PROTOCOL
section.instruction:
If you reach your context limit before completing this overhaul:
1. Immediately update BUILD_LOG.md: — Set "Active task" to the exact task ID (e.g. "D.3 — approval flow modal") — Check off all completed tasks — Note any partially-completed files
2. Create HANDOFF_NOTE.md at project root: — What was completed in this session — Exact file and function name where you stopped — What the next session should do first — Any gotchas or half-finished logic to watch for
3. Run: git add . and git commit -m "WIP: overhaul [phase] — handoff"
The next model's FIRST action is to read BUILD_LOG.md and HANDOFF_NOTE.md. It does not start from the beginning. It picks up exactly where you stopped.

closing.standard: The standard for every screen is this: A creative agency principal in Doha, Qatar — who runs a QAR 3M/year business — opens this app on their laptop in front of a client. They feel proud. Not embarrassed. The client asks: "Did you build this yourself?" The principal says: "Yes." And means it. Build to that standard.
